races.name: Tipping Tom On SBK Bet Feed Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Dream Illusion
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row earlier this year (including C&D) and runner-up on next two starts before winning at Southwell; didn't have much to spare in small field on that latest run but just 2lb higher here and clearly thriving.
runners.comment: Three wins (including C&D) from her last five starts and she's clearly thriving

runners.name: Sea Legend
runners.spotlight: Just 2-33 but both wins have come here, the latest over C&D last month; found to be lame when down the field two days later and 7f looked too sharp when third here three weeks ago; he's one to consider now back up in trip.
runners.comment: C&D win last month and 7f looked to be too sharp when third here three weeks ago

runners.name: Law Supreme
runners.spotlight: 3-6 since these cheekpieces went on, all those wins coming over 9.4f on the Wolverhampton Tapeta; drops back in trip today and was down the field on both visits here, but respected nevertheless in hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: Three wins at Wolverhampton since cheekpieces went on; different track/trip but respected

runners.name: My Awele
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at sprint trips; runner-up over 1m at Chelmsford in January and 1m2f hasn't suited on her last two starts; her reduced mark is a plus and cheekpieces are added, but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Reduced mark and return to 1m are positives but others have more pressing claims
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing CII And Partners

runners.name: Trais Fluors
runners.spotlight: His last five wins have come here, the latest off today's mark over C&D in January; however, this 12yo's subsequent form has been patchy and he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in January but this 12yo's subsequent form has been patchy

runners.name: Bajan Bandit
runners.spotlight: Continues to edge down the weights and he's well treated on the pick of this winter's course form; however, he appears to have gone off the boil on his last few outings and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Some good form here this winter but appears to have gone off the boil on last few starts

runners.name: Barleybrown
runners.spotlight: Won four times over C&D in 2024; he's on a competitive mark but drew a blank in 2025 and was merely fourth over C&D in the autumn on his last two starts; returns from a break and needs something extra.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins in 2024 but drew a blank last year and this is his first run since October

runners.name: Roger Henry
runners.spotlight: In good form for Gay Kelleway in second half of 2024 and he's 2lb lower than when going close over 1m2f here that September; market check advised on this stable debut and first run since, but it's a hefty absence to defy.
runners.comment: Went close here in September 2024 but has a hefty absence to defy on this stable debut

runners.name: Tasever
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner, including twice from the front over C&D this winter; 4lb higher than for his last win and he's never defied a mark this lofty, but the promising Harry Vigors takes off 7lb and he may make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner; has never won off a mark this high but Harry Vigors removes 7lb

races.name: Tipping Tom On SBK Bet Feed Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Alpine Sierra
runners.spotlight: 8yo who is hard to win with and he was contesting a 0-50 classified when returning to form with a close second here (1m2f) a fortnight ago; however, he's on a handy mark now back in a handicap and might not be far away.
runners.comment: Close second in classified here a fortnight ago; might not be far away now back in a h'cap

runners.name: Concert Boy
runners.spotlight: All five wins have come here, the latest over C&D in December, and he was short-headed over 7f here on March 12; this hold-up horse was on the wrong end of a pace bias when 2l sixth over C&D last Saturday and could play a leading role if this pans out more favourably.
runners.comment: All five wins have come here and he's been in good heart here of late; contender
runners.owner: Making Headway Racing And K Scott

runners.name: Eazy On The Eye
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with a bang at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) last Friday on her second run back from a break, passing the post in first (demoted; interference); gets a chance for swift compensation off the same mark.
runners.comment: First past the post at Wolverhampton last Friday; demoted (interference); same mark today

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Just 1-24 since arriving in Britain but he's twice finished second this year, including over C&D, and again holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Just 1-24 since arriving in Britain but he's twice finished 2nd this year; each-way claims

runners.name: Starliner
runners.spotlight: Course winner who ran a couple of respectable races in the winter (including over C&D) but finished last over C&D three weeks ago and has a modest 3-41 strike-rate; his claims aren't compelling.
runners.comment: Course winner but finished last over C&D three weeks ago and has a modest strike-rate
runners.owner: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt

runners.name: Chuzzlewit
runners.spotlight: Beaten on last 22 starts going back to March 2024; however, he was an encouraging fourth over C&D in February following some time off and then got no run here three weeks ago; continues to edge down weights; a possible.
runners.comment: Long losing run continues but not devoid of promise here this year; he's a possible

runners.name: Jamaican Storm
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 16 starts and needs to prove he can get the job done; however, he's finished close up when placed at Southwell (1m, Tapeta) on his last two starts and is also effective here; could be bang there.
runners.comment: 0-16 but has finished close up at Southwell on last two starts and could be bang there

runners.name: Hachiman
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) last August for the Crisfords before two lesser runs on turf; didn't run badly on recent stable debut at Southwell (1m, Tapeta) following a break; could be sharper for that outing.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who didn't run badly on recent stable debut and could be sharper today
runners.owner: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: West Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Won at Southwell (1m, Tapeta) last March and runner-up at Ayr in September on final run for Kevin Ryan; sold for 10,000GBP in October; lacks a recent outing but is not ruled out on his stable debut.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent outing but this Tapeta winner is not ruled out on his stable debut

races.name: Bet Responsibly With SBK Handicap

runners.name: Lord Capulet
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won three in a row over C&D at the start of the year and again ran well when third at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) last time; the return to Newcastle could be a plus and he's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won three in a row over C&D before good third at Southwell; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Phoenix Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Promising third when back up to 7f at Southwell last month then won over this C&D, beating Concert Boy (due to run in the 5:00) by a short head; unexposed at the trip and respected up just 2lb.
runners.comment: Short-head win over C&D recently and unexposed at the trip; respected up just 2lb

runners.name: Penny Mountain
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 16 starts but that came over 6f here on New Year's Day and she's been runner-up over this C&D on her last two starts; those last two races have been slowly run, small-field contests and she may be seen to better effect today off what should be a stronger gallop.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D on last two starts and a stronger gallop could aid her cause today

runners.name: Camera Shy
runners.spotlight: Snapped a losing sequence a fortnight ago at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) where benefiting from the strong pace to collar Anthropologist close home; unexposed at today's trip and a 3lb rise isn't harsh; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Won over 7f on the Southwell Tapeta a fortnight ago and he's unexposed at today's trip
runners.owner: The Horse Watchers 13 And Michael Dods

runners.name: Anthropologist
runners.spotlight: Modest 2-27 strike-rate but he has run just four times for this yard and went very close behind Camera Shy at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) a fortnight ago; one of his wins came here and he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but one of his wins came here and he went very close last time

runners.name: Brave Empire
runners.spotlight: Close third over 6f here in December and now 6lb lower; however, four lesser performances have followed and the return to 7f is of dubious benefit, with his three wins having been at 6f.
runners.comment: Close third over 6f here in December and now 6lb lower but four lesser runs have followed
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Ignac Lamar
runners.spotlight: Second of 14 over C&D in October and had an excuse (hampered, then lit up) over C&D in November when last seen; however, he's inconsistent and needs to be at the top of his game on this return from a break.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in October; returns from a break today and he's inconsistent
runners.owner: Lets Be Lucky Racing 35 & Adam Scott

runners.name: Bobby Joe Leg
runners.spotlight: 11-time course winner, the latest over C&D in December, and he twice finished placed over C&D last month; certainly of interest on that form but this 12yo has gone off the boil on his last two starts.
runners.comment: 11-time course winner; in good form last month but this 12yo has gone off the boil since

runners.name: Travis
runners.spotlight: Just two wins from his 29 starts but placed over 1m/7f here on his final three runs in the autumn; each-way claims if resuming where he left off.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but in good form here in autumn; e-w claims if resuming where he left off

runners.name: Novak
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner who was runner-up over C&D last month; respectable efforts over 6f here on both starts since and he could improve upon those now back up in trip; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner; runner-up over C&D last month; each-way claims now back up to 7f

runners.name: Rich Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent last year and he can have issues at the start; however, he's feasibly treated on the pick of his 2025 efforts and his trainer's horses can go well when fresh; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Feasibly treated on pick of 2025 efforts and his trainer's horses can go well when fresh

races.name: SBK: No Casino Just Sports Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Pit Boss
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D last month and placed on next four starts prior to winning over C&D last Saturday; it was an apprentice handicap last time, so he escapes a penalty today; solid contender.
runners.comment: In fine form of late and escapes a penalty for Saturday's C&D win; solid contender

runners.name: Water Of Leith
runners.spotlight: Expensive to follow since last May but returned to winning ways at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) recently; had 3l to spare and a 4lb penalty may not stop him if he can reproduce that form; has won here twice.
runners.comment: Did it in good style at Wolverhampton last week and a 4lb penalty may not stop him
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Invitorio
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in a row over C&D last September but failed to threaten here on next two starts and same story over C&D last month when back from a break; others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Twice placed over C&D last September but hasn't threatened here on his three runs since
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing Xviii And Ruth Carr

runners.name: Yorkshire Glory
runners.spotlight: He's won eight times since December, with six of them over C&D; carries 5lb penalty for Wolverhampton win under Andrew Mullen on Monday and 7lb claimer Harry Vigors, who has won on him twice, is back on board today; strong claims in his bid for a six-timer.
runners.comment: Eight wins since December, six of them over C&D, and strong claims under 5lb penalty

runners.name: Fan Mail
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who scored off today's mark at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) in October; hasn't been running badly at Southwell and Newcastle on his last few starts but needs something extra to return to winning ways.
runners.comment: Races off last winning mark; not running badly of late but needs something extra today
runners.owner: L Bond/Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi/Ptr

runners.name: Cayman Tai
runners.spotlight: Continues to drop down the weights and he popped up with a good second at Lingfield (5f, Polytrack) last month; however, he's failed to build on that on his two runs since and his losing sequence is up to 16 races.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark and good second at Lingfield last month, but hasn't built on it

runners.name: Mayo County
runners.spotlight: Merely fifth over C&D last month but this C&D winner was running well previously and has an each-way shout if leaving that latest run behind.
runners.comment: Merely 5th over C&D last month but running well previously & might have an each-way shout

runners.name: Qazaq
runners.spotlight: Off a reduced mark he returned to form when third at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) last month but failed to back that up when down the field there (7f) last time; others arrive with less to prove.
runners.comment: Back to form when third at Southwell last month but failed to back it up there since

runners.name: Kitaab
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who returned to form with staying-on fourth over 5f at Southwell last month; disappointing over this C&D since but that was a warm 0-75 and he might not be a forlorn hope now back down into a 0-70.
runners.comment: Encouraging 4th at Southwell; disappointing over C&D since but might not be a forlorn hope

races.name: SBK: No Casino Just Sports Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Sherlock
runners.spotlight: His breakthrough success over 5f here last month came on his 20th start but it was his first attempt over a sprint trip so it's possible he could build on it, particularly as his strength at the finish suggests he should be at least as effective at 6f.
runners.comment: Dropped to sprinting for first time when off the mark over 5f here latest; should go well

runners.name: Teardrops
runners.spotlight: Largely in good form over C&D this winter (three wins), bouncing back from a lacklustre effort last month when runner-up under Jack Callan three weeks ago; likely to go well again.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins this winter and another good effort here when second latest; player

runners.name: Velvet Skies
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Ireland, including one over this trip on the AW at Dundalk; placed three times there this winter and not seen to best effect (met trouble) on 6f Southwell yard debut 17 days ago; treated as if still in form.
runners.comment: In form on Dundalk's AW this winter; met trouble on yard debut so that run can be excused

runners.name: Elvetham
runners.spotlight: The drop to 6f saw him justify market support in an apprentice handicap this month; possibly found the run coming too soon when beaten favourite at Southwell a week later; 4lb higher now.
runners.comment: Second start at 6f when off mark at Kempton this month; run possibly came too soon since

runners.name: Miss Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Often front runs; visored when winning four last summer (including C&D); went close off just 1lb lower last autumn but her record when fresh doesn't obviously point to her coming out on top after six months off; ran poorly on the previous occasion she wore cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Four wins last summer (including C&D) but no great record fresh so far

runners.name: Tickets
runners.spotlight: Serial hard-puller who can miss the break and will always come with risks attached; things fell right when he won a 0-50 classified event over C&D last month but he wasn't in the same form when 5l third in a handicap back here since; first-time headgear is reached for now.
runners.comment: Things dropped right in C&D classified last month but below-par third since; headgear on

runners.name: Kings Merchant
runners.spotlight: All four wins when trained by Ed Bethell; has failed to be placed from six runs for current yard; entitled to strip fitter for his recent C&D reappearance after over five months off but others are still preferred.
runners.comment: Hasn't placed in six runs for this yard, including on recent C&D reappearance

runners.name: Pianoforte
runners.spotlight: Dips into a 0-70 for the first time but hasn't obviously been shaping as if likely to be cashing in on his reduced mark.
runners.comment: This is a drop in grade but he isn't obviously in good enough form to take advantage

runners.name: D Flawless
runners.spotlight: 1m winner for the Gosdens in May 2024; best effort for this yard came when a close third in a 5f Southwell handicap in January but not so good over 5f here since; steps back up in trip with a hood fitted.
runners.comment: Third over 5f at Southwell in January but only sixth here since; hooded first time

races.name: SBK: Betting Without The Bull Handicap

runners.name: Wicket Keeper
runners.spotlight: Pair of 6f turf wins for Ed Bethell last summer and ended his time with that stable with a close second in 6f Wolverhampton handicap in October; his new yard has made a good start to the year.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who ended last year with near miss in AW handicap; returns for new yard

runners.name: Roberto Caro
runners.spotlight: Back from three months off to finish a respectable fifth of 12 at Southwell five weeks ago; has returned to the same mark as when winning a competitive Racing League Handicap on turf last summer; dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: Back from break with a fair run last month; won competitive race off this mark last year

runners.name: Power Fizz
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f course maiden last January and went on to show useful form in turf handicaps over that trip in the spring; not seen for ten months (gelded) but should have more to offer as a lightly raced sort from a top stable; his strong-travelling, front-running style over 7f suggests he should have the speed for 6f (previous attempt came when green on debut); very interesting contender.
runners.comment: Course winner; progressive in 7f handicaps last spring; should have more to offer in 2026

runners.name: Venture Capital
runners.spotlight: Below par in two AW outings this year but might have been building up his fitness and this former course scorer is starting to look well treated on the pick of last year's turf form; no shock were he to bounce back to form.
runners.comment: Below par both runs this year but this former course scorer is back on a workable mark

runners.name: Badri
runners.spotlight: Went down by only a neck over C&D off 1lb higher in January but his two efforts since have been no more than respectable.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in January but bit below that level both starts since

runners.name: Twilight Fun
runners.spotlight: His good 5-15 AW record includes a comfortable C&D win from the front last month; creditable third of nine at Chelmsford (5f) since and the form of that race has been boosted; a must for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Good AW record includes comfortable C&D win last month; creditable third since; player

runners.name: Sergeant Wilko
runners.spotlight: Promising reappearance after wind surgery when fourth of 14 over C&D in January but failed to build on that when only sixth of seven at Lingfield (6f) four weeks ago; bounce back needed.
runners.comment: Promising fourth on C&D reappearance but only sixth of seven at Lingfield since

runners.name: Wheels Of Fire
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner who landed a 6f Windsor handicap off 4lb lower last summer; failed to reproduce that form in three subsequent outings last year but there were excuses on two of those occasions; will need a career best on reappearance (has been gelded).
runners.comment: Remains 4lb higher than Windsor win last year and career best needed on return (gelded)

runners.name: Hockney
runners.spotlight: Much improved sent handicapping after wind surgery, easily winning 0-65s over 6f at Lingfield (AW) and Thirsk (soft) last September; this represents a significant hike in grade but there's almost certainly more to come from this very lightly raced 4yo.
runners.comment: Easy wins in two 0-65s last autumn; this is a lot harder but could be plenty more to come

runners.name: Mister Bluebird
runners.spotlight: This 8yo has gained all eight career wins on turf but does have placed form on AW (including over 7f here last summer); also has a reasonable record when fresh so no surprise to see him go well for a yard that is no stranger to Newcastle success.
runners.comment: All eight wins on turf but placed here last summer and has a reasonable record fresh

runners.name: Paddys Day
runners.spotlight: Boasts a fine record over 5f here, winning in December and reaching the frame on all three outings this year; 6f AW winner for previous stable but has raced almost exclusively at the minimum trip for Nigel Tinkler; should be on the premises if still fully effective at 6f.
runners.comment: Good record over 5f here for this yard; 6f winner for previous stable; should figure

runners.name: Rousing Encore
runners.spotlight: Winner of 6f handicaps at York (good to firm) and Ayr (soft) last year; finished off with a good third in a big field at Doncaster in November; no great record when fresh.
runners.comment: Did well over 6f on turf last year, including two wins; first start for 139 days

runners.name: Twilight Calls
runners.spotlight: Losing run goes back nearly four years; no impact in sprint handicaps on turf at Meydan this year and makes a very belated AW debut on his return to Britain.
runners.comment: No win since spring 2022; struggled in Dubai this year; belated AW debut

runners.name: Andesite
runners.spotlight: Looked promising at two but didn't really kick on last year, with fifth his best placing in four handicap starts to end the season; sold out of Karl Burke's stable for 27,000gns last autumn; a watching brief is the percentage call on reappearance.
runners.comment: Useful for Karl Burke but didn't progress last year; best watched on return for new yard

races.name: Friday Night Live With SBK Handicap

runners.name: Sir Paul Ramsey
runners.spotlight: His progress stalled in the autumn but he resumed with quite an encouraging third over the extended 1m at Wolverhampton a fortnight ago; nicely treated on the form he showed in some turf handicaps at the end of last summer; needs considering.
runners.comment: Some good form last summer and recent reappearance third quite encouraging; considered

runners.name: Flying Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Gained a C&D win in January and very nearly added another three weeks ago; nudged up a further 3lb for that but he has won off his mark before.
runners.comment: C&D win in January; went close to another last time; up 3lb but still not discounted

runners.name: Urban Road
runners.spotlight: Notched three wins at the beginning of 2025 but hasn't hit the same heights this year, for all he's reached the frame a few times between 1m and 1m4f; this is competitive and his wait for another win will probably go on.
runners.comment: Respectable efforts recently without suggesting he's a winner in waiting
runners.owner: S McMillan F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Saytarr
runners.spotlight: Has taken his AW record to 4-7 when winning twice at Southwell (7f/1m) since joining James Horton; raised 6lb since last time but the way he moved into contention suggests there's likely even more to come for his new stable.
runners.comment: 2-2 at Southwell (7f/1m) since joining this yard; impressed latest and bold bid likely

runners.name: Rogue Encore
runners.spotlight: All three wins over C&D, the latest in September; failed to build on his quite promising reappearance when well beaten at Southwell five weeks ago but capable of staging a revival back at Newcastle.
runners.comment: Well held at Southwell latest but good C&D record (three wins) provides hope for a revival

runners.name: Harry The Rogue
runners.spotlight: Won only two of his 32 starts in Ireland for Andy Slattery but he did finish in the first three in half of them, including third in a 1m Dundalk claimer six weeks ago; first run for new yard.
runners.comment: Didn't win often but was regularly placed in Ireland for Andy Slattery; first run for yard

runners.name: Maestro Bernstein
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in turf maidens in Ireland for Aidan O'Brien last October; tongue-tied first time, similar form when 6l third in C&D novice for new yard last month; hard to argue his opening mark looks generous but he brings unexposed potential.
runners.comment: Placed three times (for new yard latest) and brings unexposed potential to handicap debut

runners.name: Degale
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who has progressive turf form; hasn't translated that to AW in two attempts but still early days and it's possible he needed last month's reappearance after wind surgery.
runners.comment: Progressive on turf; hasn't translated that to AW on last two starts but still early days

runners.name: Nyman
runners.spotlight: Ended his time with Henry de Bromhead with a good fourth in a Racing League handicap over C&D and struck at the second time of asking for Roger Fell in 7f Newmarket handicap in October; only nudged up 2lb and can go well if ready to roll after five months off.
runners.comment: Good fourth over C&D last summer and won on turf for new yard in autumn; respected

runners.name: Mr Mistoffelees
runners.spotlight: Reacted well to blinkers at the end of 2025, including a C&D win in October; slowly away and caught the eye with his late headway into sixth over 7f here on his final start in December; returns from 112 days off with the blinkers replaced by second-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: C&D winner last autumn; better than result when sixth over 7f here on final start

runners.name: Brewing
runners.spotlight: Four-time course scorer (6f/7f) for William Haggas; struggled on turf in Ireland for this yard last autumn but has found form again since returning to AW, placing three times at Dundalk (7f/1m) in recent months; has to enter calculations for a stable with a good record in Britain.
runners.comment: Four-time course scorer who has placed on AW in Ireland on last three starts; claims

runners.name: Izzari
runners.spotlight: 1m2f course winner in September but higher marks found him out in two subsequent visits last year; others are preferred back from four months off.
runners.comment: 1m2f course win last autumn but found out off higher marks since; off four months

races.name: SBK: Dont Settle For Less Handicap

runners.name: City Captain
runners.spotlight: Landed a fourth success of a productive first season with Harriet Bethell when comfortably seeing off 12 rivals at Pontefract in October; all those wins came at 6f but he was also second over 7f at Chelmsford last summer; no surprise were he to improve again this year.
runners.comment: Four 6f turf wins in excellent first year with Harriet Bethell; also second over 7f on AW

runners.name: Cargin Bhui
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who was successful over 6f at Wolverhampton and Southwell last month; proved himself at 7f when his strong finish only just failed to reel in the thriving Goldmoyne (won three times since) back at Southwell last time; it's likely he's still on a good mark.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last month; second to subsequent three-time winner over 7f since; good shout

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Landed a third win on AW when shading a tight finish over C&D last month but only a respectable sixth at Wolverhampton off this mark since.
runners.comment: Third AW win when shading close finish over C&D last month; only sixth since

runners.name: Flowstate
runners.spotlight: Has an excellent 4-5 record over C&D, with three of the wins coming since early February; had a bit to spare last time so a 4lb rise shouldn't prevent another bold bid.
runners.comment: His win three weeks ago took his C&D record to 4-5; looks sure to go well again

runners.name: Best Rate
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name after 16 starts; made quite an encouraging return from a short break when fourth at Lingfield in January but disappointed back there since (both 7f).
runners.comment: Bit disappointing last time, taking his record to 1-16

runners.name: Master Richard
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is a C&D winner and added another AW success at Southwell (7f) last July; ended 2025 with a good third back at Southwell (7f again) in October; formerly trained by Ann Duffield; claims if ready to roll after five months off.
runners.comment: Front-runner who is a C&D winner; ended 2025 with a good third at Southwell; off 148 days
runners.owner: T S Ingham & Mrs Liz Ingham

runners.name: Havana Prince
runners.spotlight: Spring has been quite a productive time for him which provides hope that he can build on a promising reappearance third over 6f here 15 days ago; has raced mainly at 7f/1m in recent years so the step back up in trip should help if anything.
runners.comment: Promising reappearance third over 6f here 15 days and the return to 7f should suit

runners.name: Mudamer
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run with back-to-back wins from the front over 7f at Catterick last October; found out in a higher grade at Musselburgh on final start; fifth is the best he's managed in four AW starts (all here) and it's likely best to look elsewhere on reappearance.
runners.comment: Two 7f turf wins last autumn but unplaced all four AW starts; others preferred on return

runners.name: Beale Street
runners.spotlight: All four wins over 6f here, gaining the latest when leading late on 15 days ago; placed a few times at 7f and ought to remain very competitive off only 2lb higher.
runners.comment: All four wins over 6f here, including latest start, but he does have placed form at 7f

runners.name: Eligible
runners.spotlight: No signs of age dimming his enthusiasm, completing a December/January hat-trick (second leg over 1m here); a slow start didn't help when sixth of 12 at Southwell last time and he can be treated as if still in form.
runners.comment: Now 10 but completed a hat-trick this winter and had an excuse last time (started slowly)

runners.name: Philanthropist
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; reached the frame three times in AW handicaps over this trip at end of last summer and met trouble when sixth over 1m on final start; off for 149 days but he's won off a longer break before.
runners.comment: Reappearing C&D winner who has won first time out before; claims

runners.name: Wreck It Ryley
runners.spotlight: Well backed when winning over 6f at Leicester (good to soft) last October but last of ten to Mudamer at Catterick 11 days later and off since; others are preferred.
runners.comment: 6f Leicester win last October but last to Mudamer at Catterick 11 days later and off since

races.name: SBK Betting Podcast Handicap

runners.name: Mon Na Slieve
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 5f on turf last year; also a good third over 5f on Southwell's Tapeta in September but below that level in two outings there this year, weakening quickly last time; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Good third at Southwell last autumn but below that level in two starts there this year

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: Built on his Southwell reappearance second to Master Of My Fate when seeing off five rivals (including Master Of My Fate and Reigning Profit) back there 13 days ago; compiling a good AW record and ought to go well again.
runners.comment: Solid AW record, finishing second then first over 5f at Southwell this year; big player

runners.name: Pixie Diva
runners.spotlight: Good second in competitive races in her first two handicaps last summer, latterly a Racing League event over C&D; pulled too hard when only sixth of seven on 6f Southwell reappearance but this lightly raced filly retains potential and the return to 5f looks a good move.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; good second over C&D last year; too keen on 6f return; retains potential

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Had Sports Coach behind in second when back to winning ways at Southwell (5f) last month but only a respectable fourth to that same rival back there since.
runners.comment: Beat Sports Coach to win at Southwell last month but only fourth to same rival there since
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao Ross Harmon & Partner

runners.name: Station X
runners.spotlight: Has largely run well in defeat since scoring twice over 6f on testing turf in autumn 2024, including when second of 12 on Southwell AW debut and reappearance (gelded) five weeks ago; led until approaching the final furlong at the Nottinghamshire venue so should be speedy enough to cope with the return to 5f; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Promising second on 6f AW debut and reappearance five weeks ago; enters calculations

runners.name: Blinky
runners.spotlight: Won twice on Tapeta early last year; ran well from 5lb out of the weights when second of six at Wolverhampton (5f) just before Christmas but never involved at Southwell at the beginning of January; freshened up since; same yard runs Sports Coach.
runners.comment: Good second at Wolverhampton in December but below par at Southwell since; off 82 days

runners.name: Kinetic Force
runners.spotlight: Lost his form for Des Donovan last autumn but that means he has a reduced mark to work with on this first outing for Ruth Carr after a 10,000gns sale in October; the sort this yard will gets wins from but whether it's at the first time of asking remains to be seen.
runners.comment: The sort Ruth Carr will do well with but whether it's first time up remains to be seen

runners.name: Tiriac
runners.spotlight: Had his first 30 starts on turf but reacted well to a belated switch to AW at the end of 2025, winning twice over C&D; the latter success came in quite smooth fashion so he could still be feasibly treated off 6lb higher if fully primed after 104 days off.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins belatedly switched to AW at the end of 2025; could still be feasibly treated

runners.name: Almarada Prince
runners.spotlight: All four wins when trained by Craig Lidster; drew a blank for Ivan Furtado last year but was second over 6f at Kempton on final start; faded as if his first outing for this yard was needed when eighth of 11 over 6f here 15 days ago; should last longer this time.
runners.comment: Likely needed last month's first outing for Paul Midgley and should last longer this time

runners.name: Reigning Profit
runners.spotlight: The winner of three turf handicaps in 2025, the latest off 3lb higher; stopped in his run on a couple of occasions and shaped better than the result when 2l fifth of six to reopposing Sports Coach on 5f Southwell reappearance 13 days ago; might be worth chancing.
runners.comment: Hampered and better than result behind Sports Coach on return; 3lb below last win mark

runners.name: Havana Blast
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start to his AW career in a 5f Chelmsford nursery in August 2024 and shaped well when pitched into a Group 3 over 6f at Kempton just a week later, impressing with his headway into contention before his effort flattened out (raced keenly); not seen since but retains plenty of potential as a sprint handicapper, for all it would be understandable were this outing to be needed; the betting should provide more clues in that regard.
runners.comment: Off for 566 days but displayed significant promise on AW as a 2yo; betting informative

runners.name: Vingegaard
runners.spotlight: Useful as 2yo for Archie Watson but hasn't shown he retains that level of ability in two recent comeback outings for Julie Camacho.
runners.comment: Useful as 2yo but hasn't shown he retains that ability in two comeback runs for new yard

races.name: Bet 30 Get 5 Weekly betstgeorge.com Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Broomfield Aderra
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who was competitive in bumpers there; won a maiden hurdle before finishing close up under a big weight on handicap debut at Aintree (2m4f, good to soft), and a combination of 3m on tiring ground is good reason to turn a blind eye to her no-show in a Listed race for mares at Doncaster; difficult to oppose given what she's up against here.
runners.comment: Talented mare and very difficult to oppose given what she's up against here

runners.name: Ka Mate Du Brivet
runners.spotlight: Bought for 62,000GBP after winning an Irish point on yielding to soft in November; offered little in a Southwell bumper (soft; 10-1) but highly likely that he's capable of better than that.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; disappointing in a bumper but surely he's better than that

runners.name: Willshebe Court
runners.spotlight: No show in a couple of bumpers and was 100-1 when pulled up on her hurdling introduction at Plumpton.
runners.comment: No show in bumpers; 100-1 when pulled up on her hurdling introduction at Plumpton

races.name: Join Bet St George "Hands And Heels" Handicap Hurdle (ConditionalsAmateurs) (Racing Excellence)

runners.name: Motazzen
runners.spotlight: Four Flat wins and three over hurdles; has yet to win this season over jumps but two of his three placed efforts came here and he's likely to be a strong contender, for all that last two runs (hurdle/AW) weren't his best.
runners.comment: Last two runs (hurdle/AW) weren't his best but his form here heightens interest

runners.name: Geordies Betty
runners.spotlight: No match for an easy odds-on winner in her latest maiden and those behind weren't a great bunch; however, she's lightly raced and this marked step up in trip could benefit on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and this marked step up in trip could benefit on her handicap debut

runners.name: Kylenoe Dancer
runners.spotlight: Second run back from an absence of 679 days when the decisive winner of a 2m3f handicap here in May of last year (good ground); now back from another layoff but probably has it in her to cope with the 6lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Back from another layoff but a winner last time and still lightly raced; raised 6lb

runners.name: Vendant
runners.spotlight: Very nearly won over C&D last January and that was only three starts ago; feasible excuses since then as probably failed to stay 3m1f and then bled here when last seen a year ago; ability respected but there are wellbeing issues after so long out.
runners.comment: Ability respected but there are wellbeing issues after so long out

runners.name: Wot You Wearing
runners.spotlight: He was looking when regressive even before pulling up in his first two runs for this yard but ran better at Taunton (2m3f, good; ridden by Kayla Ruddy), where he was no match for the front two but did come from well off the pace; possibilities; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: He was looking when regressive but did run better to finish a 14l third at Taunton

runners.name: Peking Opera
runners.spotlight: Listed Flat winner for Aidan O'Brien; made a successful hurdle debut for this yard in early 2024 but it's been a struggle since then under both codes and he's tough to trust at the minute.
runners.comment: Has back-class but he's been struggling for a while now under both codes
runners.owner: S Packham A Austin And B Austin

runners.name: Godot
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner but arrives here in no sort of form and the new blinkers (replacing usual cheekpieces) did nothing for him two weeks ago; now goes in no headgear; risky.
runners.comment: Three-time winner but arrives here in no sort of form and all headgear now left off
runners.owner: A Lewers D Shaw A Irvine

races.name: betstgeorge.com Top Prices On English Events Handicap Chase

runners.name: Kitsilano
runners.spotlight: The ground was heavy for his hurdle win in Ireland (2m3f) and when scoring by 8l at Leicester two runs ago (2m), opening his chase account after wind surgery; ran okay off this 7lb higher mark at Plumpton (soft) latest but drying conditions might not be favourable.
runners.comment: Two good runs after a wind operation but drying ground might not be favourable

runners.name: Ronnies Reflection
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come in the mud at Leicester where he ran another respectable race three weeks ago (good to soft); has C&D form and needs respecting.
runners.comment: Consistent gelding with C&D form and needs respecting

runners.name: Valirann Gold
runners.spotlight: This is a better trip than the 2m2f he ran over here last time though could still have run better; the new blinkers tried then are absent and so too the cheekpieces that he had been wearing; even when on a good day he's very hard to win with (2-41 under rules).
runners.comment: Even when on a good day he's very hard to win with (2-41 under rules)

runners.name: Daring Plan
runners.spotlight: 7lb higher when going close over 2m3f here last May (good) but remains a maiden and has become inconsistent.
runners.comment: The odd run offers hope but remains a maiden and has become inconsistent
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Tom Desjy
runners.spotlight: Completely tailed off in three non-handicap hurdles but now makes chase/handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces and he was beaten only 2l by the smart Moneygarrow in his only Irish point; could easily take a huge step forward.
runners.comment: Nothing in three runs over hurdles but point form reads well; chase/handicap debut

races.name: England To Win Tonight 20/1 betstgeorge.com Maiden Hurdle (Novices' Champ Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Unknown Entity
runners.spotlight: Ex-Willie Mullins; eye-catching third on stable debut at Ludlow (2m, good to soft), then went close at Wincanton and wasn't disgraced in the always-strong EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f, soft) 20 days ago; provided he accepts the two new forms of headgear he should be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Major form claims and hard to beat if accepting the two new forms of headgear

runners.name: Grizzled Squirrel
runners.spotlight: Advanced his RPR despite finishing last of the nine runners in a bumper at Ascot where he was only 7l off the winner; should have a future over hurdles.
runners.comment: Advanced his RPR despite finishing last of the nine runners in a bumper at Ascot
runners.owner: Galloping On The South Downs Partnership

runners.name: Im Wide Awake
runners.spotlight: Bumper efforts make for better reading than his two tailed-off efforts over hurdles; one for another day.
runners.comment: Bumper efforts make for better reading than his two tailed-off efforts over hurdles

runners.name: Last Round
runners.spotlight: Second in an Irish point; beaten 33l and 51l in two runs over hurdles, though the latest was behind Sober Glory at Newbury; this isn't anything like so demanding as that race was.
runners.comment: Beaten 33l and 51l in two runs over hurdles, though the latest was behind Sober Glory

runners.name: Graeme The Grey
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit in bumpers, though was no closer than 16l in any of them; best watched on this hurdling introduction.
runners.comment: Showed a bit in bumpers, though was no closer than 16l in any of them

runners.name: The Quant
runners.spotlight: Never threatening and tailed off in a soft-ground Chepstow bumper two months ago (16-1).
runners.comment: Never threatening and tailed off in a soft-ground Chepstow bumper two months ago (16-1)

runners.name: Jambon
runners.spotlight: Held every chance when falling in his third Irish point; has shown some promise under rules (bumper/maiden) without suggesting he'll be troubling Unknown Entity.
runners.comment: Held every chance when falling in third Irish point; has shown some promise under rules

runners.name: Trooper
runners.spotlight: Had plenty of Flat racing on the continent, going 5-25; 33-1 and tailed off in a good-ground maiden hurdle at Taunton in January and has since left Tom George.
runners.comment: 5-25 on the Flat; 33-1 and tailed off on hurdle debut when in a different yard

runners.name: Khalkeau Spigao
runners.spotlight: A disappointing favourite when beaten 20l in a juvenile hurdle here in November (good to soft); market strength would imply that he's no doubt capable of better.
runners.comment: A disappointing favourite when beaten 20l in a juvenile hurdle here in November
runners.owner: D S Dennis & John Hw Finch

races.name: betstgeorge.com With Daily St George Specials Chase Series Final (A Handicap Chase) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Alright Dai
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles prior to his successful chase debut over C&D (soft) and when a 6l third off this mark at Sedgefield his jumping could have been better; needs considering.
runners.comment: C&D winner; when a 6l third off this mark at Sedgefield his jumping could have been better

runners.name: The Gypsy Davey
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner who has been running better than his recent heavy defeats may imply and he's on a good mark with Harry Cobden booked; off since October.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner who has been running better than his recent heavy defeats may imply

runners.name: Koenigsstern
runners.spotlight: Can be a bit in and out but he stuck to his task and just got the better of Le Galli Bier for second last time at Plumpton (2m4f, soft); however, the winner left both behind and this 9yo is off the same mark.
runners.comment: Stuck to his task and just got the better of Le Galli Bier for 2nd last time at Plumpton

runners.name: Rip Wheeler
runners.spotlight: Has won three of his six starts over fences and perhaps found the ground too soft when a tired second behind Mon Champion over C&D recently, as he looked threatening until late on; this surface brings him into it.
runners.comment: Tired behind Mon Champion last time but this drier surface brings him into it

runners.name: Superstylin
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut for Chris Gordon in September and has continued to run well in defeat; this longer trip shouldn't be a problem but he has been edging up the weights and he's 2lb out of the handicap here.
runners.comment: Consistent but he has been edging up the weights and he's 2lb out of the handicap here

runners.name: Mon Champion
runners.spotlight: Back on song when a 13l winner from Rip Wheeler over C&D two weeks ago, seeing it out stronger than the second on the soft ground; the returning cheekpieces did him no harm and a clear contender despite going up 6lb; all three wins have been on soft or heavy.
runners.comment: A 13l winner on soft here last time and a 6lb rise could have been worse

runners.name: Le Galli Bier
runners.spotlight: Twelve defeats divided equally between hurdles and fences; hasn't been finding masses in the finishes of his races and he's 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Hasn't been finding masses in the finishes of his races and he's 2lb wrong

runners.name: Alien Storm
runners.spotlight: Back from a break with three excellent efforts and he's only 3lb higher than when winning at Doncaster the last time (November; 2m3f, good to soft); has run well on his two previous visits here.
runners.comment: Consistent in victory and defeat this season; has run well on his two previous visits here

races.name: Bet St George Supports Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Juggernaut
runners.spotlight: Has been running respectably in defeat over fences and was runner-up off 2lb higher on his final hurdle start in August; if gelling with new rider James Robottom then shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Has been running respectably in defeat over fences and he's a capable hurdler

runners.name: Hello Cello
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m3f handicap hurdle here in October under Ciaran O'Shea (good to soft) and chasing hasn't gone to plan since then; respected back in this discipline as she's not handicapped out of it.
runners.comment: Chasing not gone to plan; back over hurdles and won over C&D in October
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Hello Cello

runners.name: Bridge
runners.spotlight: Sole success in 18 starts came in a maiden here in 2023; went close back here on December's return from a mammoth absence and his fourth to Tommy Dillon two runs ago wasn't too bad a run; does seem hard to predict, however.
runners.comment: A chance on some of his form but he's looking a hard horse to predict

runners.name: Tommy Dillion
runners.spotlight: Tailed off last time but had appreciated the return to hurdles with a second here (beaten 16l) before a 3l success at Lingfield (2m, heavy); he's able on good to soft ground if he can shrug aside that poor last run.
runners.comment: Poor latest run but was second then first in two races on his return to hurdling

runners.name: Dr Waksman
runners.spotlight: Has finished runner-up on the Flat and over hurdles but has been struggling under both codes since returning from more than three months off; the new cheekpieces will need to make a difference.
runners.comment: Has been struggling under both codes and new cheekpieces need to make a difference

runners.name: Mistress Fox
runners.spotlight: Has no obvious form claims; 50-1 for handicap debut last time and ran accordingly.
runners.comment: Has no clear form claims and she was 50-1 for handicap debut last time and ran accordingly

runners.name: Test The Market
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in an Irish bumper and again in his two runs over hurdles; latest Stratford conqueror isn't a bad sort and this 5yo brings potential into handicaps after a break.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both hurdle races and this 5yo brings potential into handicaps

runners.name: Jacquetta
runners.spotlight: Uncompetitive in his first three races, although it was a competitive novice he contested at Newbury (2m, beaten 45l; 200-1) in January; pulled up at Lingfield (2m4f, heavy) latest; the market can guide on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Uncompetitive in his first three races; the market can guide on handicap debut

races.name: #wearahatday Supporting Brain Tumour Awareness Month Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pioneer Pete
runners.spotlight: Weakened into modest third over 2m3f at Catterick last time; consistent at 2m otherwise in bumpers and over hurdles; holds solid and leading claims back down in distance.
runners.comment: Has consistent form at 2m; holds solid and leading claims back down in distance

runners.name: Chico Magnifica
runners.spotlight: Has failed to shine in two attempts over hurdles and may be more interesting in handicaps shortly; otherwise retains potential judged on Flat record (2-4; best RPR 85).
runners.comment: Record of 2-4 on Flat; has failed to shine in two hurdles attempts

runners.name: Emerald Time
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two bumpers but looks less solid on hurdles form that comprises a late fall (6l down at the time) and poor effort; however, certainly has ability so could go well.
runners.comment: Made the frame in bumpers; less solid on hurdles form

runners.name: King Esoteric
runners.spotlight: Has a smart Flat pedigree; showed some promise under that code in sole French start; failed to finish in Doncaster maiden hurdle (2m) but should be capable of better; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Failed to finish at Doncaster (British/hurdles debut) but should be capable of better

runners.name: Collanisi
runners.spotlight: Regressive RPRs since debut promise (bumper); modest fifth to stablemate at Southwell (2m) most recently; turnaround is more likely to occur in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Turnaround is more likely to occur in handicaps shortly

runners.name: Double Tiger
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced and nicely bred 7yo who gives the impression he's likely to improve back down in distance with 2m4f reappearance under his belt; interesting in this second hurdles attempt.
runners.comment: Likely to improve back down in trip with 2m4f reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Hashtag Queen
runners.spotlight: Regressive form in bumpers; failed to beat a rival last time; needs major improvement for the switch to hurdles and fitting of hood.
runners.comment: Regressive form in bumpers; hood fitted

runners.name: Finbarr
runners.spotlight: Both starts at Newcastle, finishing fourth in bumper then pulled up (seemingly not beaten entirely by the 2m4f trip) in novice hurdle; still needs to prove he can back up the debut form.
runners.comment: Still needs to prove he can back up debut form (bumper)

runners.name: Prince Phil
runners.spotlight: Took a good hold, despite wearing a hood, but managed to show some promise with fifth of nine in Kelso bumper; may improve, especially if settling better in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Showed some promise with fifth of nine in Kelso bumper; may improve

runners.name: Our Guide
runners.spotlight: Prevailed by just under 1l in Galway bumper (yielding) in sole start for James Conheady; form well advertised by the third since; joined new connections for 82,000GBP; one of the main players.
runners.comment: Galway bumper winner; joined new connections for 82,000GBP; one of the main players

runners.name: Asta La Vista
runners.spotlight: Promise shown in good-ground bumper at Worcester has not been backed up in two maiden hurdles on softer going; turnaround is possible returned to forecast faster conditions.
runners.comment: Turnaround is possible returned to forecast good ground

runners.name: Prince Of Clova
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced 5yo who has a similar profile to Finbarr; chance depends on how well he responds to first-time hood.
runners.comment: Similar type to Finbarr; hood applied
runners.owner: Clova Farms And P D Niven

runners.name: Hellion
runners.spotlight: Finished third in a couple of Flat races for previous yard but lacked fluency and trailed home last of nine at Catterick on the switch to this code; tongue-tie applied.
runners.comment: Trailed home last of nine on hurdles debut

runners.name: Millford Hill
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-5 in Irish points; poor form in two attempts over hurdles.
runners.comment: Poor form in two hurdles attempts

runners.name: Betteryouthanme
runners.spotlight: Fairly exposed maiden pointer in Ireland and never figured at Ayr (2m) on the switch to rules.
runners.comment: Never figured on rules debut

races.name: Watch The Grand National On Racing TV Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Youdecide
runners.spotlight: Point/hurdles winner who hasn't strictly improved in two chase attempts but may build on latest effort (3m) as he remains unexposed at this distance; visor replaces the cheekpieces worn of late; in the mix.
runners.comment: May build on latest effort as he remains unexposed at this distance; in the mix

runners.name: Slaney Opera
runners.spotlight: Point and hunter chase winner who is still unexposed in handicaps; heavy ground provides an excuse for latest effort; scored at Sedgefield (3m2f) the time before and remains of interest.
runners.comment: Heavy ground provides an excuse for latest effort; still unexposed in handicaps

runners.name: Cardamon Hill
runners.spotlight: Scored off slightly higher marks twice, including over C&D, in November 2024 when under the care of Dianne Sayer; returns from 13-month absence; market may prove best guide.
runners.comment: On a handy mark but has been absent for 13 months; market helpful

runners.name: Mixedwave
runners.spotlight: Takes on possible improvers in this field; otherwise a solid candidate who has five chase wins on his CV, the most recent off current mark in this race 12 months ago; last two efforts show he's back in form; respected.
runners.comment: Five chase wins, the most recent off current mark in this race last year; solid claims

runners.name: Ithaka
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in both starts since opening his chase account in Hereford contest; still seeking first success above Class 5; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Pulled up in both starts since opening his chase account in Hereford contest
runners.owner: K P Marsh Plastering & Interiors Ltd

runners.name: Holeshot
runners.spotlight: Mixed results (5FP4) since switched to chasing but is now only 1lb above last winning hurdles mark and Edward Vaughan takes off a bonus 7lb; possibilities, provided he stays returned to 3m.
runners.comment: Only 1lb above last winning hurdles mark and Edward Vaughan takes off bonus 7lb

runners.name: The Great Escape
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden whose hurdles form has dipped since a consistent spell; however, this combination of a switch to fences and first-time headgear may have a positive effect; bred to make a chaser.
runners.comment: Combination of switch to fences and first-time headgear may have a positive effect

runners.name: Giovanni Change
runners.spotlight: Veteran who was a close second in this contest last year but is by no means certain to go one better, having gained all six jumps wins at Market Rasen; inconsistent results since last success.
runners.comment: Ran well in this race last year but has gained all jumps wins at Market Rasen
runners.owner: Readers & Wiggy The 8 Amigos & J Burns

runners.name: The Clonmel Kid
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but may benefit from this switch to chasing, combined with first-time headgear, and looks attractively handicapped off current mark judged on some of his Irish point form; interesting.
runners.comment: May benefit from this switch to chasing; attractively treated on Irish point form

races.name: Albert Bartlett Triple Crown Series Handicap Hurdle Qualifier)

runners.name: Duel Au Soleil
runners.spotlight: Maiden prior to showing improvement with solid performances (132) since having wind surgery and wearing cheekpieces, most recently beaten only narrowly at Ascot (2m7f) three months ago; that form has been franked by the winner since; goes well fresh and holds strong claims returned to the scene of his October win.
runners.comment: Solid performances (132) since having wind surgery and wearing cheekpieces; big player

runners.name: Ionian
runners.spotlight: Represents the Constitution Hill connections; record is 0-5 but his two placed efforts, both over 2m, suggest there may be a race of this nature in him; however, doesn't appear to be crying out for this step back up in distance on handicap debut.
runners.comment: May have a race in him but doesn't appear to be crying out for this step back up in trip

runners.name: Forest Blaze
runners.spotlight: Below par at Ayr last time, ending a consistent spell of form; rebound looks possible but this is a difficult task and he's still seeking a first win away from Hexham.
runners.comment: Faces a difficult task and has never won away from Hexham
runners.owner: M Blades Craggs Clark And Cambridge

runners.name: Miss Cynthia
runners.spotlight: May still have more to offer for Dan Skelton but is by no means certain to follow up a fortuitous win at Fakenham (2m4f) where she was gifted the race when the clear leader exited; now 1lb higher in a stronger event.
runners.comment: By no means certain to follow up a fortuitous win at Fakenham; this is a stronger race

runners.name: Freshers Week
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo whose broadly solid record includes point/hurdle wins; placed at Haydock (3m) on handicap debut most recently, while shaping as if this return to shorter will suit; likely to perform well.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 5yo who has a broadly solid record; likely to perform well

runners.name: Jhentong Enki
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 7yo who is 2-3 for new stable, the wins in Class 5 handicaps at Lingfield (2m3f) and Leicester (2m4f) in December; now 7lb higher and upped in grade but may well improve further; helps to give Ben Pauling a robust hand.
runners.comment: 2-3 for new stable, the wins in December; upped in grade but may well improve further

runners.name: Celestial Reign
runners.spotlight: Gained sole success in Sedgefield maiden; holds weak claims on his form since switched to handicap hurdles; yard appears to have a better chance with Mandarb.
runners.comment: Holds very weak claims on his form since switched to handicap hurdles

runners.name: Daylatedollarshort
runners.spotlight: Lacked fluency but knuckled down well to prevail by a neck in C&D handicap on Boxing Day when last seen, adding to his Irish point win and taking overall record to 2-4; open to further improvement, especially if jumping better; respected.
runners.comment: Knuckled down well in C&D handicap on Boxing Day and is open to further improvement

runners.name: Dirty Den
runners.spotlight: Gained his Plumpton success (2m4f) in small field and was only midfield in a deeper race at Wincanton later in February; opposed in another competitive contest.
runners.comment: Gained his Plumpton success in small field; only midfield in deeper contest since

runners.name: Assertive Walk
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden whose form dipped in Ayr handicap (2m4f) most recently; however, he's in good hands and may do better still; remains attractively treated on his first couple of efforts.
runners.comment: Form dipped last time (handicap debut) but he's in good hands and may do better still
runners.owner: Mrs P Bond Magniers Mrs P Shanahan

runners.name: Dunkerque
runners.spotlight: Successful on Flat in Denmark and over hurdles in Ireland; not disgraced behind the market leader in 2m4f novice event at Sedgefield (second start for new yard) most recently; this is a much deeper race.
runners.comment: Not disgraced last time (second start for new yard) but this is a much deeper race

runners.name: Bras dOr
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage maiden whose form (point/bumper/hurdles) stacks up well; debut in this sphere was behind a horse who has since run well in the Turners; open to progress now handicapping over a new trip with tongue-tie fitted; interesting contender who could be on a very workable opening mark.
runners.comment: Form stacks up well; open to progress now handicapping; could be on a very workable mark

runners.name: King Of The Road
runners.spotlight: Soft ground provides an excuse for two defeats of late; sole attempt in cheekpieces resulted in good second at Kempton (2m5f) on penultimate start; could go well in the reapplied headgear.
runners.comment: Sole attempt in cheekpeices resulted in creditable second at Kempton on penultimate start

runners.name: Keno Black
runners.spotlight: Benefited from first-time hood at Ludlow (2m) in latest attempt in maiden hurdles; may build on that success and show further progress in the retained headgear, provided he's as effective back up in trip.
runners.comment: Took well to first-time hood in Ludlow maiden over 2m; may build on that success

runners.name: Just Call Me Lucy
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in 2m3f mares' handicap most recently, taking Wetherby record to 3121; faces a stiffer assignment in this field, back against males, but holds solid claims kept to this venue.
runners.comment: Last-time win took her Wetherby record to 3121; brings solid claims to this stronger race

runners.name: Mandarb
runners.spotlight: Registered an easy success in Class 5 contest at Doncaster (2m3f) on handicap debut when last seen four months ago, opening his hurdles account; now 12lb higher and upped in grade but may be capable of further progress in this sphere; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Registered an easy success at Doncaster on handicap debut when last seen; not ruled out

runners.name: No Ordinary Joe
runners.spotlight: Has spent most of his career in better handicaps; on a long losing spell and 0-8 for current yard but shaped encouragingly on penultimate start; possibilities off current mark dropped sharply in grade.
runners.comment: Has spent most of his career in better handicaps; possibilities dropped sharply in grade

races.name: Bulloughs Cleaning Services Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gaelic Rambler
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage maiden who has broadly consistent form figures; clear second at Stratford (2m1f) most recently; possibilities dropped further in distance, provided he takes well to added cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden; possibilities, provided he takes well to added cheekpieces

runners.name: Singapore Trip
runners.spotlight: Somewhat similar to Zero Tolerance, being an exposed sort whose chase record is 0-8; the difference is that he's a five-time hurdles winner, most recently off 5lb higher at Wetherby (2m3f) in October; recent form suggests this drop in trip is worth exploring; interesting back here.
runners.comment: Gained last hurdles win off 5lb higher at Wetherby; interesting back here

runners.name: Zero Tolerance
runners.spotlight: Well capable of being involved, as his close third at Catterick on most recent attempt over 1m7f attests; the negative is that he's a fairly exposed maiden whose latest defeat took chase record to 0-8.
runners.comment: Well capable of being involved but he's a fairly exposed maiden

runners.name: Koapey
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo; solid record until let down by his jumping on last appearance (second chase attempt); had wind surgery (his third) since and now goes in tongue-tie; still open to further progress, provided he avoids errors.
runners.comment: Still open to further progress, provided he jumps better than in latest start

runners.name: Parramount
runners.spotlight: Bumper/Flat/hurdles winner; on a handy mark but his form has dipped in 2026, so this switch to fences needs to have a reviving effect; latecomer to this game, being a 10yo.
runners.comment: On a handy mark but this belated switch to fences needs to have a reviving effect

races.name: Watch Racing TV In Stunning HD Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ballygeary
runners.spotlight: Has inconsistent form for current stable but ran encouragingly last time (2m5f) while shaping as if this return to further is worth exploring; well treated on historical data, which includes wins off higher marks in 2023 and 2024.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly last time; remains well treated on historical data

runners.name: Jalisco Star
runners.spotlight: In-form staying mare whose wins in December/February flanked second-place finishes; made the frame at Newbury most recently; may no longer be a step ahead of the assessor but still holds reasonably solid claims.
runners.comment: In-form staying mare; perhaps weighted to the hilt but holds solid claims

runners.name: American Mike
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly in a Pertemps qualifier most recently (second start for new yard) but is interesting off current mark dropped in class, being so attractively treated on the pick of his form for Gordon Elliott.
runners.comment: Drops in class looking attractively treated on the pick of his form for Gordon Elliott

runners.name: Jupiter Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo whose two attempts for Nicky Richards, sandwiching a spell with a different yard, comprise a Wetherby bumper success on debut and excusable run (lost chance when hampered) in Pertemps qualifier last time; may yet resume his progress.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 7yo whose latest effort is best forgiven; may yet resume his progress

runners.name: Kent De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Successful twice in Irish points and once over hurdles for Rebecca Menzies; ran creditably at Kelso (2m6f) last time on handicap debut; market may offer further guidance in this first start for Sam England.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner for Rebecca Menzies; makes debut for Sam England

runners.name: Thank You Maam
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his chase/hurdles form this year; previously a consistent sort in this sphere and has a fighting chance off current mark back down in grade, provided he's resurgent.
runners.comment: Not solid on his chase/hurdles form this year; previously a consistent sort
runners.owner: The Posh Pundit Racing Club Limited

runners.name: Escapologist
runners.spotlight: Seems badly in under a penalty for recent 2m7f success at Market Rasen, with future mark 3lb lower; however, remains unexposed at this sort of distance and may show the progress that's needed to follow up.
runners.comment: Seems badly in under a penalty for his Market Rasen win but remains unexposed at about 3m

races.name: Malton Marquees Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Farmer Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over C&D in November on soft (effective on good) but hasn't kicked on from there and not sure the latest Sedgefield run (3m3f) was that much of a step back in the right direction.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D in November on soft (effective on good) but hasn't kicked on from there
runners.owner: J Scarrow I Barker & S Turner

runners.name: Rapid Mission
runners.spotlight: Flat winner but hardly progressing over hurdles (0-8) and this longer trip is a change in direction.
runners.comment: Hardly progressing over hurdles (0-8) and this longer trip is a change in direction

runners.name: Moveonbuy
runners.spotlight: Cartmel winner who nearly doubled her tally over C&D in January; needs to leave subsequent efforts behind.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in January but needs to leave subsequent efforts behind

runners.name: Yummy
runners.spotlight: First effort of note over hurdles when a keeping-on fourth (beaten 13l) on handicap debut at Doncaster (2m, soft) and this longer trip could make a difference according to her pedigree.
runners.comment: Slightly better run on handicap debut and she's raised markedly in trip here

runners.name: Rebel Intentions
runners.spotlight: Two good runs over 3m1f at Catterick earlier in the season but form has gone cold; others preferred.
runners.comment: Two good runs over 3m1f at Catterick earlier in the season but form has gone cold

runners.name: King Casey
runners.spotlight: No wins in 11 but was close up over C&D (soft) in January and 3m3f perhaps stretched him next time; possible.
runners.comment: Close up over C&D (soft) in January and 3m3f perhaps stretched him next time; possible

runners.name: Ck OMara
runners.spotlight: Contesting only his second handicap when the well-backed winner of a 3m soft-ground handicap at Ayr, where he was going away at the finish; up 5lb and they claimed 7lb last time so has more on his plate at these weights, but he looks an improver.
runners.comment: Recent Ayr winner; has more on his plate at these weights, but he looks an improver

runners.name: Jonquilla
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in her two qualifying races, giving the useful Silver Hill (a winner since) something to think about in the first; Sean Bowen is booked for this handicap debut and this trip should be within range.
runners.comment: Two encouraging seconds over hurdles and Sean Bowen is booked for handicap debut

runners.name: Wild Nephin
runners.spotlight: Point/hurdle winner who has tried fences, including on his last two runs; it's now 12 months since his last effort of any note.
runners.comment: Back over hurdles but it's now 12 months since his last effort of any note
runners.owner: K P Marsh Plastering & Interiors Ltd

runners.name: Eire Street
runners.spotlight: Too risky when he's pulled up in four of his last five races and over two years since he last won.
runners.comment: Too risky when he's pulled up in four of his last five races

runners.name: Getaway Glory
runners.spotlight: Case can be made on pick of his form over hurdles and fences but it's been miserable stuff in three handicap chases since returning from 485 days off; all headgear removed this time.
runners.comment: It's been miserable stuff in three handicap chases since returning from 485 days off

runners.name: Jeteye
runners.spotlight: Defied a similar mark in June 2024 for sole career success (2m7f, good); ran well afterwards but now returns from a very long absence.
runners.comment: In decent heart in 2024 and feasibly treated, but has been absent for a long time

runners.name: Twoplacesatonetime
runners.spotlight: No stamina doubts having won over nearly 3m at Hexham but this season's efforts leave him with much to prove.
runners.comment: 2m7f winner around Hexham but this season's efforts leave him with much to prove

runners.name: Bodygroove
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m here last March but nothing in two runs this season and there are stamina doubts; the return of a hood (fitted for that win) could help.
runners.comment: 2m winner here; nothing in two runs this season and there are stamina doubts

runners.name: Steady The Ship
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and has been pulled up in his last three races, all handicap chases; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles and has been pulled up in his last three races, all handicap chases

races.name: Thank You All Wetherby Groundstaff #groundsweek Open National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)

runners.name: Noble Way
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with 15l third of eight at Doncaster (soft) while leaving the impression he'll likely be suited by switching to faster ground; the pick of the runners with bumper experience.
runners.comment: Showed promise with third of eight at Doncaster; the pick on rules form

runners.name: A Sight To Sea
runners.spotlight: Comfortably justified favouritism in Irish point in November and sold for 190,000GBP soon after; not a bumper type on breeding but otherwise brings potential to rules racing; interesting.
runners.comment: Comfortably justified favouritism in Irish point; sold for 190,000GBP since; interesting

runners.name: Matador St Georges
runners.spotlight: 84,000euros 3yo; sibling to four winners, notably Aterisk (French 1m7f-2m1f hurdle including Listed); dam French 1m4f Flat/2m2f hurdle scorer; major stable; respected deubutant.
runners.comment: 84,000euros 3yo; sibling to four winners; major stable; respected debutant

runners.name: Cape Warrior
runners.spotlight: 8,000GBP 3yo; sibling to winners George Ridsdale (Flat/hurdle) and Chicago Lightning (Flat); dam 1m4f-1m6f scorer; yard's bumper strike-rate is modest.
runners.comment: 8,000GBP 3yo; sibling to two winners; yard's bumper strike-rate is modest

runners.name: Middlecliffe
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off last of 12 at Huntingdon for an inauspicious debut.
runners.comment: Tailed-off last of 12 at Huntingdon

runners.name: Cricket Hill Denis
runners.spotlight: Out of a bumper winner but was among the outsiders and ran accordingly in C&D event last week for a weak debut.
runners.comment: Among the outsiders and ran accordingly in C&D event last week

races.name: Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Bronte Beach
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when keeping on from well off the pace in a 1m handicap at Kempton last month; ridden more positively upped to 1m2f at Chelmsford 15 days ago and a reluctance to settle contributed to her weak finishing effort; dropped 3lb and still not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Promising handicap debut; too keen upped to 1m2f latest; still has some potential

runners.name: Magic Runner
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Wolverhampton (9.4f) this winter and she is on the same mark as for the latest success; wide draw to deal with but she is usually held up; not discounted.
runners.comment: Two Wolverhampton wins (9.4f) this winter; on a competitive mark; tricky draw to deal with

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: Usually seen over further; down in the weights and comes here on the back of two solid efforts over 1m4f; 2lb ahead of the handicapper but his rider is still seeking a first winner.
runners.comment: 2lb well in after a good 2nd (1m4f) last week; rider seeking her first winner under rules

runners.name: Baloos Blues
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but mildly progressive at up to 1m; stamina the query today but he should be able to find a small race in the coming weeks.
runners.comment: 0-7 but mildly progressive at up to 1m; stamina the query today but can find a small race

runners.name: Latin
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a Wolverhampton hat-trick in January; solid run of form ground to a halt when racing too freely upped to 1m4f last week; a hood now replaces a visor; not much room for error off this mark but a better run is expected all the same.
runners.comment: Wolverhampton hat-trick in January; too keen over 1m4f last week and now tried in a hood

runners.name: One Million Dreams
runners.spotlight: Little to celebrate over hurdles this winter but he finished second (1m4f here) on his mos recent Flat start; cheekpieces added today; has won for today's rider.
runners.comment: Quiet over hurdles this winter but ran well on his most recent Flat outing; new headgar

runners.name: Under The Sun
runners.spotlight: He had been badly out of sorts prior to popping up at 80-1 in a C&D handicap four weeks ago; still has handicapping scope up 3lb but it remains to be seen if he can back that effort up.
runners.comment: Sprung an 80-1 surprise over C&D four weeks ago; still well treated if he can back it up

runners.name: Volto Di Medusa
runners.spotlight: Both his wins have come over a mile and he has a modest record in races over further than 8.6f; ran well for third at Wolverhampton this month though and he has a top amateur booked today; new blinkers replace usual cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Two 1m wins to his name; good third off this mark 25 days ago; new headgear today

runners.name: Zambezi Magic
runners.spotlight: Prolific at Ffos Las in the mud but just 1-40 away from that track; easy enough to look elsewhere now returning from an absence and from a wide stall.
runners.comment: 7-15 on Flat at Ffos Las but just 1-40 elsewhere; easy enough to swerve after an absence

runners.name: Crafter
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when fourth of eight at Chelmsford a fortnight ago (blinkered first time; ridden by Jack Loughnane); dropped another 3lb since; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Fair 4th tried in blinkers at Chelmsford two weeks ago; 3lb lower today; each-way shout

runners.name: Molly Valentine
runners.spotlight: C&D win last April came off a 4lb lower mark (blinkered first time); ended her time with Dean Ivory quietly though and ridden by an inexperienced amateur on this stable debut.
runners.comment: C&D winner last April; out of sorts when last seen; best watched on stable debut

runners.name: Lennys Spirit
runners.spotlight: Much better record on turf than AW and he has finished out of the frame in all seven runs on the AW track here; starts out for Adam Kirby (same owner) off a reduced mark but others are more appealing today.
runners.comment: Better record on turf than AW for former stables; others appeal more today

races.name: Bet Â£10 Get Â£40 With BetMGM Handicap

runners.name: Far Too Fizzy
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; made all to win over 6f in fine style in January; similar form when third over 6f here four weeks ago; one to consider off the same mark.
runners.comment: 6f win here in January and he ran well over that same C&D last month; should go well

runners.name: Wisetanck
