runners.spotlight: Bumped into one over C&D on his stable debut last month and made no mistake at Wolverhampton two weeks ago (cheekpieces first time), keeping on strongly; this is a lower grade and a 3lb rise shouldn't prevent a big run.
runners.comment: Strong finish to win at Wolverhampton two weeks ago; up 3lb but down in grade; good chance

runners.name: Mister Moet
runners.spotlight: Steadily run race at the trip didn't play to his strengths when a rallying second of six at Newcastle two weeks ago; could again be found wanting for pace at this sharper track.
runners.comment: 6f course win last month; rallying 2nd dropped back to 5f latest; sharper track this time

runners.name: Marra Donna
runners.spotlight: Three solid efforts since moving into handicaps this year, finishing third on each occasion (7f and twice over 6f); has a bigger effort in him at some point; this drop to 5f is worth exploring and new accessories could give him a lift.
runners.comment: Third in three handicap runs this year (6f/7f); drops in trip with accessories added

runners.name: Solar Invincible
runners.spotlight: 5f win in Class 6 company in December; fair third over C&D last month but that effort will need bettering if he is to come out on top.
runners.comment: 5f AW win in December (Class 6); fair third over C&D last month; needs improvement

runners.name: Sands Of Seve
runners.spotlight: Failed to live up to market prominence in his first three handicaps; showed his true colours fitted with a visor at Wolverhampton three weeks ago, making all and coming home clear; up 9lb and he is taking on stronger opposition but further progress is possible in this headgear.
runners.comment: Much improved for a visor when bolting up at Wolverhampton latest; up 9lb in better race

races.name: Daily Profit Boosts At betmgm.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: One More
runners.spotlight: 7f novice win at Kempton last August and he showed better form in two handicaps there last autumn; down the field in two runs in Dubai this year but he looks a key player back on British soil.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW last year; quiet in Dubai this year but this looks more suitable

runners.name: Dandy Khan
runners.spotlight: She has enjoyed a productive winter for her new connections, winning five races here; last week's third (1m), while respectable, came in a lower grade against other females and she looks vulnerable today.
runners.comment: Had a fine winter but she seems to have peaked and this is a rise in class

runners.name: No Return
runners.spotlight: Raced over sprint trips since 2024 but did stay this far as a 2yo; mixed bag for Charlie Johnston this winter but his best run came at this track; others look stronger.
runners.comment: First run over this trip since his 2yo days; mixed bag for new yard this winter

runners.name: Giorgio M
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of December's stable debut (6f) by making all over C&D two weeks later; also third over 6f here last month; only seventh at Wolverhampton since but his Lingfield record affords him respect.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D in December; below par at Wolverhampton latest but respected back here

runners.name: Spirit Lead Me
runners.spotlight: Won twice in four days last September (7f and 1m) and he is well handicapped on that form; yet to shine this winter though and may need a drop in class and/or the return of cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won twice on AW last September; quiet this year and may need an ease in grade

runners.name: Way To Dubai
runners.spotlight: A drop to 5f backfired last month but he was quickly back in the groove when a close third of ten over C&D last week; plugged on over 6f here on Wednesday and should be happier back up in trip.
runners.comment: Several good course runs this winter; return to 7f will suit; each-way shout

runners.name: Brunel Nation
runners.spotlight: He has run just once at this track and it resulted in his best performance for some time, finishing a neck second over this trip in January; not so good at Kempton last month but he drops back down in class today.
runners.comment: One course run and it yielded a close second in January; struggled in a Class 3 last time
runners.owner: R Lane L Watson & J Langridge

runners.name: Metallo
runners.spotlight: Ran well for second in a 1m2f handicap here on last month's stable debut; failed to build on it at Wolverhampton last week and drops back to 7f with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Good 2nd (1m2f) on February's stable debut; couldn't back it up at Wolverhampton last week

runners.name: Sergeant Pep
runners.spotlight: Twice a runner-up over 6f this winter, including here, but he raced too freely in one 7f run and he still has to prove he needs this far.
runners.comment: Placed on his last two starts but he still has to prove his stamina for this trip

races.name: Read Meg Nicholls' Blog At betmgm.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: My Shagaf
runners.spotlight: No improvement for handicapping yet but she has finished third in three of her last four starts; cheekpieces now added but when tried in blinkers she pulled hard and was well beaten.
runners.comment: Has shown promise but pulled hard and was well beaten when headgear was tried in January

runners.name: Arjack
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress from his debut effort in three subsequent 2yo runs, including his nursery debut when last seen in October; dropped 4lb but he returns with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Didn't progress from his debut effort as a 2yo; still early days but improvement is needed

runners.name: Mighty Vega
runners.spotlight: Ran as well as he ever has when second of seven, beaten a neck, in a 6f handicap at Newcastle last week; 2lb ahead of the handicapper and Kieran O'Neill replaces an apprentice; still to prove he needs 7f though.
runners.comment: Good 2nd at Newcastle last week (6f); 2lb well in but has more to prove over 7f

runners.name: Roccobear
runners.spotlight: He has only beaten two rivals home in his three runs in maiden/novice company; marked improvement needed for the step up in trip.
runners.comment: Beaten just two rivals home in his three maiden/novice starts; opposable on handicap debut

runners.name: Okiru
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of his five starts, including when chasing home the red-hot favourite at Newcastle five weeks ago (7f; My Shagaf third); won't need much improvement for the new cheekpieces to play a starring role.
runners.comment: Placed in three of his five starts; cheekpieces now added; more appealing than many

runners.name: Because We Can
runners.spotlight: Improved to make a winning handicap debut at Wolverhampton in January (7f; visored first time) and that in spite of pulling hard; again failed to settle when fifth of nine back at Wolverhampton eight weeks ago; still has time to do better.
runners.comment: 7f win on h'cap debut; again pulled too hard latest; can do better if learning to settle

runners.name: Pretty Danielle
runners.spotlight: Six runs as a 2yo, showing her best form when third of 12 at Windsor last July (second start); no threat in two nurseries but the handicapper is easing her burden and she still has time on her side.
runners.comment: Fair form as a 2yo but she was no threat in either handicap start; may do better this year

runners.name: Yachtsman
runners.spotlight: Creditable third on his stable debut at Wolverhampton in January (6f; hooded first time, absent today); still to prove his stamina for 7f; tongue tied today; withdrawn after breaking out of the stalls at Kempton this month.
runners.comment: Creditable 3rd on stable debut (6f) in January; stamina for 7f still to be proven

runners.name: Jamie Sommers
runners.spotlight: Won a modest 7f fillies' novice at Wolverhampton in December, her third start, but absent since a low-key run on her stable debut one month later; improvement required.
runners.comment: Novice win came in a modest event; no impact on handicap debut; improvement needed

runners.name: Obsidian Verse
runners.spotlight: Shaped nicely on his second 2yo start (6f, good to soft); gelded after latest run; up in trip for his handicap debut and he looks a likely improver.
runners.comment: Up in trip for his handicap/seasonal debut; been gelded; open to plenty of progress

races.name: BetMGM Supports Safer Gambling Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Thunderhoof
runners.spotlight: Short of room at a crucial stage when third of eight on last month's Southwell debut (7f); kept on steadily when in the clear and he is one to take seriously with that experience behind him.
runners.comment: Promising 3rd at Southwell on last month's debut (7f; met trouble); open to  improvement

runners.name: Stella Lucente
runners.spotlight: Outran her 40-1 odds when a close second over C&D on debut last September; that form isn't anything out of the ordinary but it gives her something to build on this year.
runners.comment: Promising 2nd over C&D on debut 205 days ago; needs more but that is quite possible

runners.name: Dusk Damsel
runners.spotlight: Two well-beaten fourths this month (7f/1m) and a good jolt of improvement will be required to win.
runners.comment: Two well-beaten fourths this month (7f/1m); a jolt of improvement will be required to win
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Scheffler
runners.spotlight: 32,000euros foal, 62,000euros yearling, second foal of an unraced half-sister to winners Muharaaj and Come On Dave; blinkered for debut; betting to guide.
runners.comment: 62,000euros yearling; dam unraced half-sister to two winners; blinkered; betting to guide

runners.name: Montague Menace
runners.spotlight: Has a BHA mark of 79 but three runs last season yielded a peak RPR of 62; gelded since last seen; open to improvement now switching to AW and his stable is in fine form.
runners.comment: Ran to modest RPRs in three 2yo runs but gelded over the winter and may do better

runners.name: Marveling
runners.spotlight: Sent off at big prices and finished down the field in two 7f runs last year; bred to do better but such progress may come when handicapping.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two 7f runs as a 2yo; may not come into her own until handicapping
runners.owner: John Troy And Mrs Rebecca Philipps

runners.name: Menwirr
runners.spotlight: 9-2 third favourite for his debut in a Newcastle novice (7f) last September, weakening in the closing stages to finish eighth of the nine runners; gelded during his six-month absence; bred to be useful.
runners.comment: Weakened into 8th of 9 on sole 2yo start (7f); gelded since; bred to do much better

races.name: Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Handicap

runners.name: Secret Road
runners.spotlight: Two wins over shorter trips for James Owen in January; impressive over C&D on his stable debut three weeks ago (cheekpieces first time) and he looks a strong contender despite a 5lb rise.
runners.comment: Impressed over C&D on stable debut three weeks ago; up 5lb but unexposed as a miler

runners.name: Fools Rush In
runners.spotlight: Well short of his best on his last three starts but he won off this mark at Southwell in early February (7f); capable on a good day but this isn't sure to be run to suit.
runners.comment: Won off this mark last month (7f) but three lesser efforts have followed
runners.owner: S Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Poke The Bear
runners.spotlight: Disappointed over 7f here four weeks ago but this trip probably suits better and he isn't handicapped out of things judged on his previous second here; wouldn't be ideally suited by a tactical event.
runners.comment: Three course wins this winter; not at best last time but he looks the type to bounce back

runners.name: Royal Jet
runners.spotlight: Five wins since December, three of them at this track (including over 1m); 3lb wrong under his penalty but he's never been in better form.
runners.comment: Five wins since December, three of them here; not well in under his penalty but respected

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Southwell off this mark in January; not at quite the same level more recently and 7f perhaps more his trip.
runners.comment: 7f win off this mark in January but blown hot and cold since then; trip a nagging doubt

runners.name: Always Fearless
runners.spotlight: Promising return to action at Kempton this month and duly stepped forward when seeing off eight rivals at Wolverhampton (8.6f) one week later; up 4lb but wouldn't look handicapped out of things.
runners.comment: Comfortable win at Wolverhampton (8.6f) 18 days ago; up 4lb but still feasibly weighted

runners.name: Sharma dAmour
runners.spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut over C&D last April when rated 3lb higher; failed to progress afterwards but she may be refreshed by a break and a revival wouldn't be a complete bolt from the blue.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 3lb higher last April; no progress after but may be refreshed by a break

runners.name: Youarenotforgiven
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a drop in the weights when winning at Wolverhampton last week (8.6f; ridden by Billy Loughnane); 4lb penalty to shoulder but he won off a higher mark as recently as October; one of two solid contenders for Tony Carroll.
runners.comment: Ready winner at Wolverhampton last week (8.6f); leading player under a penalty

runners.name: Cosi Bear
runners.spotlight: She finished second at Southwell (7f) on her second 2yo run but there are question marks over the value of that form; should come on for last month's return from a long absence and stepping up in trip should be a help judged on her pedigree; unexposed.
runners.comment: Should come on for last month's return from a long absence; 1m will suit; unexposed

runners.name: My Ambition
runners.spotlight: Beat Royal Jet over C&D last September and he is 19lb better off with that rival today; has won after a break in the past and he is worth a market check.
runners.comment: Beat Royal Jet over C&D last September & 19lb better off today; market useful after break

races.name: Make The Move To Midnite Handicap

runners.name: Fornido
runners.spotlight: Won at Southwell (1m6f, Tapeta) in October and went close behind Forglen over this C&D in January; returned to form with 2l fourth to Forglen at Southwell (1m6f) a fortnight ago and may well be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in January and ran well when fourth at Southwell latest; thereabouts

runners.name: Forglen
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in January; runner-up at Southwell (1m6f) next time and has won over 1m6f at Wolverhampton and Southwell on his two starts since; his wins have been narrow ones, meaning he is climbing the weights only slowly, and there can be optimism this progressive 5yo has more left in the tank.
runners.comment: Three wins from last four starts; there can be optimism he has more left in the tank

runners.name: High Favour
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 4yo who is hard to fancy on the evidence of his last two starts (1m4f, including here); however, before those he made some late headway over 1m4f here on his stable debut, hinting that today's step up in trip might be a good move; a hood goes on; possibly not a forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on the face of it but lightly raced & the longer trip is an interesting move

runners.name: Charles Morin
runners.spotlight: Beaten just a head over 1m4f here last month on his last Flat start and he ran well over 2m on turf here last July; didn't perform badly back over hurdles last week; might not be far away now reverting to the Flat.
runners.comment: Beaten just a head over 1m4f here on last Flat start and he's probably okay at this trip

runners.name: Sneaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: Bounced back from Chelmsford flop (1m6f, Polytrack) to win at Wolverhampton (2m, Tapeta) last time; his last three wins have come there but he ran well on his previous AW outing here and is entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton last time and ran well on sole previous AW outing here; respected

runners.name: Jack Langley
runners.spotlight: Two wins here (1m4f/1m5f) towards the end of last year, which sandwiched a close third of five at Chelmsford on his sole previous crack at 2m; continued in good form over 1m4f here on his last two starts and he's on the shortlist now back up in trip and kept to his favourite venue.
runners.comment: All three wins have come here and he ran pretty well on his sole previous attempt at 2m

runners.name: The Craftymaster
runners.spotlight: Below par at Newcastle last time but in good form previously, finishing third over this C&D before winning at Chelmsford (1m6f); has won twice over today's C&D and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Below par at Newcastle last time but in good form previously; two wins here; not ruled out
runners.owner: R Miles V Miles T Stamp & Partner

runners.name: Sullivan Bay
runners.spotlight: Ran well in fourth at Chelmsford (2m, Polytrack) in January when back from some time off and built on that to go close there (1m6f) four weeks ago; there's every chance he'll be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Went close at Chelmsford four weeks ago and every chance he'll be bang there once more

runners.name: The Corporate Guy
runners.spotlight: Encouraging third at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) last month on third handicap start but down the field at Southwell (1m3f, Tapeta) since and he could do with settling better now up in trip.
runners.comment: Promise two starts ago but down the field last time and could do with settling better

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: His three wins have come over 1m6f/2m on AW (including Polytrack) and he's dropped to a basement mark; however, he was again well beaten here on Wednesday (1m4f) and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Down to a basement mark but he was again well beaten here on Wednesday

runners.name: Izakaya
runners.spotlight: Won her last two Flat starts in the autumn (2m1f/2m, soft) and she was running well when falling three out on her hurdle debut at Fontwell in December; this is her first run since and she's struggled on AW previously, but she could be a different proposition now; wears first-time hood; could go well.
runners.comment: Progressive on soft ground last autumn; could go well if return to AW isn't an issue

runners.name: Balgowan
runners.spotlight: Twice finished placed here last year (2m/1m4f); not ruled out each-way but this is his first run since October so he may improve for it, and he's won just one of his 27 starts.
runners.comment: Twice placed here last year but returns from a break and has a poor strike-rate

races.name: Dundalk Stadium Group Deals Available Apprentice Optional Claiming Race

runners.name: The Liffey
runners.spotlight: Seeking a hat-trick of claiming wins here after wins at a mile and 6f last month, latest under today's rider last month, overcoming tardy start when coming late; intermediate trip ideal and the one to beat again.
runners.comment: Seeking a hat-trick of claiming wins here; the one to beat again

runners.name: Dontspoilasale
runners.spotlight: Well-backed when landing a third win here (all at 6f) in January; nearly 3l off stablemate The Liffey in 6f claimer two starts back, most recent handicap run was okay; rare foray over 7f here and could be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Best form at 6f, could be vulnerable at this trip

runners.name: Una Matata
runners.spotlight: Excellent overall record in a busy campaign since joining Ado McGuinness in November, winning four of her 11 starts, three times at this trip; didn't land a blow here a week ago from wide draw in handicap but has to be respected back in claiming company.
runners.comment: Didn't land a blow a week ago from wide draw in handicap but respected back in a claimer
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Mark McGuckin & Claire Wylie

runners.name: Crest Of Light
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner in Britain at around 1m but regressed and having changed hands for 2,400gns soundly beaten in both hurdling attempts over the winter; wouldn't be out of it if running to his British AW form.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in maiden hurdles; wouldn't be out of it if running to his British AW form

runners.name: Benavente
runners.spotlight: Five of six wins have come here at distances between 1m and 1m4f; well held last twice and takes a marked drop in trip and yard seemingly have a stronger contender in The Liffey.
runners.comment: Wins have been over further; yard seemingly have a stronger contender in The Liffey

runners.name: Thaloria
runners.spotlight: Stepped up markedly on maiden runs when scoring over C&D on handicap debut in January; poor effort following month off 7lb higher mark in similar contest; bit to find on ratings but not to be ruled out.
runners.comment: C&D handicap winner; bit to find on ratings but not to be ruled out

runners.name: Ruff Justice
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 6f turf maiden and a 7f handicap as a 2yo for Warren Greatrex in 2024; didn't progress in his 2025 and having been bought for 4,000gns in October ran okay for a long way on yard debut here over a mile last month; drop in trip to suit and place claims.
runners.comment: Ran okay for a long way on yard debut here over a mile last month; drop in trip to suit

runners.name: Suerte
runners.spotlight: Standout run came when touched off in 6f maiden here in January 2025; not so good on turf and well held in C&D handicap debut when last seen in November; hood now added to tongue-tie tried last time; stiff task at weights.
runners.comment: Well held in C&D handicap when last seen in November; hood added; stiff task at weights

races.name: New Saddle Bar At Dundalk Stadium Handicap

runners.name: Bishopton
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden last summer before following up over the same trip in Down Royal handicap; back to that level of form on latest when strong-finishing 1/2l off an all-the-way winner here over 6f in December; back up to career-high mark but return to 7f to suit and respected.
runners.comment: Recent second here; back up to career-high mark but return to 7f to suit and respected
runners.owner: Nigel OHare/D W Sloan/Milena C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Synd

runners.name: Blue Anthem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (two on AW), signing off with a 6f AW second at Wolverhampton; bought for 21,000gns in October and keen on yard debut over C&D three weeks ago, weakening final furlong; expected to come on for that and 2lb lower now.
runners.comment: Expected to come on for C&D yard debut run 3 weeks ago and 2lb lower now

runners.name: Nikki Swango
runners.spotlight: Five-time course winner (four at this trip), most recently in December off 73; good second (not clear run) in quite valuable Newcastle handicap following month but needs to bounce back from underwhelming C&D effort three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Five-time course winner but needs to bounce back from underwhelming C&D effort 3 weeks ago
runners.owner: Claire Wylie & Mark McGuckin & Mark Devlin

runners.name: Musical Act
runners.spotlight: Won two Kempton AW novice events at 7f/1m in 2023; close up in Grade 3 at Keenland in April 2024; C&D fourth last month his best effort for new connections, didn't appear to get home over a mile latest; return to 7f to suit and yard going well; cheekpieces worn last twice now discarded.
runners.comment: Return to 7f to suit and yard going well; cheekpieces worn last twice now discarded

runners.name: Venetian
runners.spotlight: Four AW wins up to an extended 1m1f, twice over C&D including in January off 74; kept busy since over various trips, most recently best work at finish after a slow start (stalls slow to open) when beaten 1.5l over 6f; return to 7f to suit and okay mark so one to consider.
runners.comment: Return to 7f to suit and okay mark so one to consider

runners.name: Spinning Around
runners.spotlight: Went close in Roscommon maiden last summer and off the mark final start on AW/handicap debut over C&D in October, coming home well when holding two stablemates in tight finish; 5lb higher now but type to keep progressing (will want 1m in time) and likely big player (yard had a winner here last week).
runners.comment: Absent since C&D win in October; 5lb higher but big player; yard had winner here last week
runners.owner: Mrs M V Magnier Mrs OLouglin Mrs Byrne

runners.name: Whistling Jamesie
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced at two, getting off the mark (20-1) final start on AW debut in 6f handicap here in October, having shown plenty of speed from outside draw and asserting late; gelded since and likely open to more improvement at this longer trip; 2lb wrong but has to be considered.
runners.comment: 6f winner in October; 2lb wrong but 3yo likely open to more improvement so respected

runners.name: Um Wadi
runners.spotlight: Debut AW winner in France at two and fair form first half of last season on softish ground; bought for 28,000euros in July and after promising yard debut at Leopardstown in September, reportedly had blood in both nostrils when running poorly at Navan following month; first AW run since August 2024.
runners.comment: French AW winner absent since bursting blood vessels in October at Navan
runners.owner: Mark Feighery & Kevin Blake & Paul McKeon

races.name: Sunday 12th July Ladies Day At Dundalk Handicap

runners.name: Son Of Wind
runners.spotlight: 1-14; well handicapped on 2yo form but didn't get going in 2025; dropped into 0-60 race at Wolverhampton (5f) when last seen in October, finishing a 3.75l fifth of ten; makes stable debut in first-time hood but others more appealing.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on 2yo form but didn't get going last year; makes stable debut

runners.name: Winemaker
runners.spotlight: Added his fifth win (third on AW) when winning cosily over 6f here in January off 5lb lower (has won off a mark of 72 before); hampered when attempting to follow up over this C&D next time, finishing a 2.5l fourth; not at his best back on turf at the Curragh last time (heavy); cheekpieces on now and claims if on song.
runners.comment: Added fifth career win over 6f here in Jan'; hampered in C&D follow up attempt; claims

runners.name: Manhattan Dandy
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner but 0-21 on AW; 1.25l second over C&D in December off 1lb higher; finished 1l behind Roman Harry over 1m here last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner is 0-21 on AW; 1l behind Roman Harry here over 1m latest

runners.name: Vibrato
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner at 7f/1m for Jennie Candlish with his last win coming at Southwell off 70 in August; not at the same level since, but the best of three runs for this yard came last time here over 5f when 3l behind the winner; into 0-60 company now and returns to winning distance.
runners.comment: Four-time British AW winner; enters 0-60 company now and returns to winning distance

runners.name: Deep Vein
runners.spotlight: Creditable enough run at Clonmel in September when sixth (9.5f, soft); 1m4f was perhaps too far here last time when weakened into ninth; capable of more.
runners.comment: Creditable sixth at Clonmel in Sept'; 1m4f perhaps too far here last time; capable of more

runners.name: Jasmine Affanalis
runners.spotlight: Ended the season on a positive note when making the breakthrough at the eighth attempt in a Tipperary handicap (1m1f, yielding); much more needed based on 5.5l tenth last time at 66-1; however, entitled to improve on that comeback run and back in 0-60 company now.
runners.comment: Made breakthrough at eighth attempt in final run last season; poor on seasonal return

runners.name: Cosmic Force
runners.spotlight: A 13-race maiden; placed only once (in a small-field race for George Boughey); best form in 2025 was in claimers; not beaten far in sixth over 6f here two weeks ago off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: 13-race m'den, placed just once; not beaten far in sixth over 6f here two weeks ago

runners.name: Roman Harry
runners.spotlight: Gained just second win from 38 starts when springing a 50-1 shock in a C&D claimer in December; again ran with credit over 1m last time when a 1.75l fourth on stable debut; place chance.
runners.comment: Gained just second win from 38 starts in a C&D claimer; creditable fourth last time

runners.name: Sakakawea
runners.spotlight: Sligo 5.5f maiden winner in 2024; has adapted well to this surface with creditable efforts in four starts; took a 2l third over C&D last time (nose behind Comfort Line); runs off the same mark and Keane rides again.
runners.comment: 2024 Sligo m'den winner; creditable efforts in four runs here inc' when third latest

runners.name: Jazzy Dancer
runners.spotlight: Made all when winning over C&D in February off 50; favourite to follow up over 6f last time when headed inside the final 110 yards; nudged up another 1lb but rider claims 4lb more than previously; he's in good form and has won off a mark of 62 in the past.
runners.comment: Made all in C&D win in Feb'; headed late on last time; has won off higher marks in past

runners.name: Carrickfinn
runners.spotlight: First reserve; maiden after 20 starts; close fourth over 1m last July; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Reserve; maiden after 20 starts; close fourth over 1m last July; 2lb wrong

runners.name: Comfort Line
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner with his last win coming over C&D this time last year off 72; beaten 3.5l behind Jazzy Dancer in February; ran on for a 2l second last time; can get involved.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner; ran on for 2l second over C&D last time; can get involved

runners.name: Methgal
runners.spotlight: Good showing over 1m here in January when a 3.25l fourth of 14; not seen at his best upped to 10.5f when last seen; this trip can help.
runners.comment: Good 3.25l fourth in Jan' over 1m; not at his best upped to 10.5f latest

runners.name: Qvee
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in good ground maidens over 7f/1m; wore tongue tie for heavy ground Gowran handicap debut (now left off) when a very disappointing last of 14 (tailed off); hood goes on now for AW debut.
runners.comment: Minor promise in m'dens; tongue tie left off following tailed off Gowran h'cap debut

runners.name: Hot To Foxtrot
runners.spotlight: Very little mileage on the clock; definite promise in two of her maiden runs, the first of which was in 2023; however, her three handicap attempts have been poor, big price when in rear over C&D in December; drops into 0-60 race for the first time.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; promise in m'dens but poor in h'caps; enters 0-60 race for first time

races.name: Mid-Week Easter Break Racing Wednesday 1st April Handicap

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Two of his seven wins came over 2m on AW in Britain; at the veteran stage now and held by several of these on recent form at this venue.
runners.comment: Two of seven wins came over 2m in Britain; well held by several of these on recent form

runners.name: Slowdownbarney
runners.spotlight: 1-22 with his win coming comfortably over 1m6f at Wolverhampton off 59 in April last year; not much encouragement in outings at this track but a visor was reapplied (retained) at Wolverhampton last time and ran his best race for some time (although still a 3.25l fifth of seven); others preferred.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner at Wolverhampton last year; not much encouragement in recent runs

runners.name: Disco Boy
runners.spotlight: Maiden produced much less encouraging runs last summer after a good 1m6f Gowran effort ; made seasonal return over 1m4f here last timr when 4.25l behind in eighth (denied a clear run from 2f out); others more appealing.
runners.comment: M'den followed good Gowran run last summer with poor efforts; eighth here last time

runners.name: Deluca Chop
runners.spotlight: Last win came over 10.5f here in 2023 off 58; didn't race in 2024; signs of return to form in recent runs, placing in his last three starts; has shown a tendency to hang but big player upped to this trip for first time since 2022
runners.comment: Last win came in 2023; placed in his last three starts; big player.

runners.name: Sarmiento Power
runners.spotlight: 1m4f course winner in September off 13lb lower; some good efforts following that, including when left with plenty to do and running on for fifth when last seen in November (10.5f); his win came following a break and he's a big player if this new trip gets some more from him.
runners.comment: His 1m4f course win came following a similar break and big player if getting this trip

runners.name: Black Soul
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 21 starts came in an extended 2m1f maiden hurdle at Down Royal in May (good) for John and Thomas Kiely; 0-16 on the level; makes AW debut now but others preferred.
runners.comment: M'den hurdle winner is 0-16 on the level; makes AW debut now but others preferred

runners.name: Numidia
runners.spotlight: Dour stayer gained third course win over this trip in November off 52; decent runs in defeat over 1m4f following that; ran keenly when a 8.5l sixth back over C&D in January; player if settling better.
runners.comment: Gained third course win over this trip in Nov'; too keen last time; player if settling

runners.name: Circus Act
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner went close over C&D in 2023; decent fourth at Sligo in May (13.5f, good); tailed off back from 292-day break in a Leopardstown mares' hurdle earlier this month; not out of it at her best.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner was a decent fourth at Sligo in May; not out of it at her best

runners.name: Jazz Forever
runners.spotlight: Beat Numidia by 2.25l here over 10.5f in his sole win from 27 starts in 2024; generally poor form in 2025; decent 4l fourth here in January (10.5f) but beaten 28l over hurdles last time; tries this trip on the Flat for the first time.
runners.comment: One win from 27 starts; generally poor form in 2025; decent fourth here in Jan'

runners.name: Hobart
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in four Flat runs; has been running over hurdles recently and went very close in a Down Royal maiden hurdle in August (2m4f, good to firm); tailed off at Navan when last seen (2m, soft to heavy); makes AW debut now.
runners.comment: Unplaced in four Flat runs; some decent efforts over hurdles recently; makes AW debut

runners.name: Mephisto
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in 2023; racked up three back-to-back handicap hurdle victories last summer; below his best in two September runs when last seen; should give a good account of himself back here.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in 2023; three h'cap hurdle wins last summer; can go well

races.name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Lamar River
runners.spotlight: Never sighted in recent Curragh maiden, likely best watched.
runners.comment: Never sighted in recent Curragh maiden, likely best watched

runners.name: Belle Island
runners.spotlight: From second crop of smart sprinting 2yo Ubettabelieveit; cost 10,000gns as a foal and 18,000GBP as a yearling; half-sister to four winners inc Emilia James (5f 2yo; RPR 85 and Together Aclaim (10.4f turf/10.6f AW; 83); dam 5f 2yo winner (98); one of two for the yard, market best indicator to her chances.
runners.comment: 18,000GBP yearling; half-sister to a 5f winner; one of two for the yard, market best guide
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell

runners.name: Brazilian Diva
runners.spotlight: Earthlight filly cost 12,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Kardinya (9.4f AW; RPR 72), Hopeforthefuture (Italian 5f 2yo) and Biodreina (Italian 1m 2yo); dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Khor Dubai (6f/7f) and Stormardal (1m-9.7f); yard's debutante juveniles always worth a market check.
runners.comment: 12,000euros yearling; yard's debutante juveniles always worth a market check

runners.name: Maria Quiteria
runners.spotlight: Churchill filly cost 25,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to US 5.5f turf winner Navy Chief; dam 1m/9.4f winner (inc AW; RPR 84), half-sister to winners Point North (5f-1m) and Thomasgainsborough (1m 2yo); one of two for the yard, market likely best indicator to her chances.
runners.comment: Half-sister to US 5.5f turf winner Navy Chief; one of two for the yard, watch market

runners.name: Whammy
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear filly cost 28,000euros as a foal (25,000GBP buy-back); second foal; dam French 7.5f AW winner (inc 2yo), closely related to prolific Japanese 5f-7f winner Peaceful Time, out of French 1m Group 2 winner; yard's juveniles can go well here and market worth a check.
runners.comment: 28,000euros foal; yard's juveniles can go well here and market worth a check

races.name: BAR 1 Betting "Join Our Weekly Rewards" Maiden

runners.name: Tyraxes
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden went close in two January maiden runs, behind an experienced sort over 1m4f and only caught late on by a neck over 10.5f in first-time cheekpieces; something surely amiss when tailed off in a 1m4f handicap last time (reported to be post race normal); tries this shorter trip for the first time, but thereabouts at his best.
runners.comment: Went close in two January m'den runs; tailed off in h'cap last time; thereabouts at best

runners.name: Wild Sapphire
runners.spotlight: Very promising Curragh debut in 2024 when 0.5l behind subsequent Dewhurst runner-up Expanded; beaten 1l in a Gowran maiden in April last year on seasonal return (1m, yielding); decent fourth in a 1m1f Leopardstown maiden when last seen in August (good); gelded since then and top chance if sufficiently ready.
runners.comment: M'den efforts make him the one to beat; top chance if sufficiently ready

runners.name: Mystic Bella
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; hasn't built on promising Gowran debut when a 4.5l third of ten over a mile; well beaten over C&D last time in a new headgear combination which is now discarded; something to prove.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on promising turf debut; well beaten over C&D last time; something to prove

runners.name: Gaiety Girl
runners.spotlight: Placed in two 7f maidens here; caught the eye when staying in late at 33-1 on debut for third and filled same position next time; second on handicap debut over C&D last time; reliable sort but a bit to find on ratings with Wild Sapphire.
runners.comment: Placed in two 7f m'dens here before runner-up on C&D h'cap debut latest; reliable

runners.name: Grecian Power
runners.spotlight: Outran 33-1 odds on belated debut in a 7f Curragh maiden in July, missing the break and staying on nicely for third; failed to build on that at Fairyhouse next time; her Curragh run would give her a definite chance here.
runners.comment: Outran odds when third on Curragh debut but poor next time; definite chance at best
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Pedro The Poet
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in opening two bumper runs; decent fourth over 1m4f here in February but didn't build on that at the Curragh (1m, heavy) last time when 29l behind.
runners.comment: Tailed off in two bumpers; decent fourth here in Feb' but 29l behind on turf last time

runners.name: Good Onya Mate
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; poor debut effort at Roscommon in September when tailed off (1m4f, soft); improved for switch to AW last time when an 8l fifth of 14 (10.5f); others more appealing.
runners.comment: Improved for switch to AW here last time when fifth; others more appealing
runners.owner: P J Morgan & Mrs S L Morgan

runners.name: Poets Willow
runners.spotlight: Last of ten at 150-1 in her debut over 1m4f here in February; ruled out
runners.comment: Last of ten at 150-1 in her debut over 1m4f here; ruled out

runners.name: Pure Valour
runners.spotlight: Beaten 28l in a Curragh m'den 12 days ago at 80-1; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Beaten 28l in a Curragh m'den 12 days ago at 80-1; hard to fancy

runners.name: Barretstown
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner (three over C&D) with his last win coming in September off this mark; best of three 2026 runs came here two weeks ago when 0.75l behind Storm Eric; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Won off this mark in Sept; 0.75l behind Storm Eric last time; shortlisted

runners.name: Star Mind
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner in Britain but never a factor in seven tries at this course; others more appealing judged on recent efforts, although he now drops into this lower grade.
runners.comment: British AW winner but never a factor in 7 tries here; drops into this lower grade

runners.name: Storm Eric
runners.spotlight: Three course wins at 7f/1m and is now 2lb below his last winning mark from January last year; knocking on the door here recently, placing in four of his last five runs (three over C&D); took third in a higher grade last week; leading chance.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door here recently with multiple placed efforts; leading chance

runners.name: Rising Sky
runners.spotlight: Consistent if limited maiden; positive 2025 efforts including here, finishing fourth over 1m twice; upped to 1m4f here last time when never better than midfield; tongue tie and blinkers replace the previously worn cheekpieces now; place chance.
runners.comment: Consistent if limited m'den; positive efforts here in 2025; place chance

runners.name: Captain Hanley
runners.spotlight: Maiden is 0-16; a handful of good efforts recently including when a 1.5l second over 1m4f last time in this headgear; Colin Keane rides for the first time now; big player.
runners.comment: 0-16 but a handful of good efforts recently inc' when second latest; Keane rides now

runners.name: On The Cards
runners.spotlight: 0-13 and triple-figure odds for his last six runs (two of those in handicaps at this track); hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-13; triple figure odds for last six runs (two of those in course h'caps); hard to fancy

runners.name: Palazzo Persico
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner for Ed Dunlop (three on AW); won four-runner Brighton handicap last July; some encouragement in his 5.75l seventh here in February, but that is sandwiched between two poor efforts for new yard.
runners.comment: Four-time winner for Ed Dunlop; poor efforts in two of three starts for new yard

runners.name: Harbanaker
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner at 1m off higher marks than this; weakened into an 8l seventh when last seen here in January over C&D; 3lb wrong but on a dangerous mark if returning to form.
runners.comment: Dual 1m course winner off higher mark than this; beaten 8l over C&D when last seen

runners.name: Rushford
runners.spotlight: Two wins in Britain in 2022 came at 1m4f/1m6f on quickish ground; returned from a 578-day absence over 1m4f here last time when last of 14; hard to fancy based on that.
runners.comment: Last of 14 back from lengthy absence here last time; hard to fancy based on that
